summary: This is a brief promotional post tied to Mother's Day 2007. Joel uses the holiday as a hook to remind readers that Copilot (Fog Creek's remote desktop assistance tool) is free for the day. He suggests readers use it to help their mothers switch to Firefox and install security patches remotely. The post is essentially a lighthearted product plug wrapped in a holiday greeting.
key_points: Fog Creek's Copilot remote assistance tool was offered free on Mother's Day as a promotional event
Joel frames tech support for family members as a relatable, universal experience
The post implicitly advocates for Firefox over Internet Explorer, reflecting mid-2000s browser preferences
money_quotes: Copilot is free today, so here's your chance to switch your mom over to Firefox and install all the latest patches.
key_insight: Joel leveraged holidays as natural promotional moments for Fog Creek products by tying them to relatable experiences like providing remote tech support to family.
